title: Texas Agricultural Producers Assistance Act
plain_summary: This bill tells the Secretary of Agriculture to write a report about help for Texas farmers. Mexico is supposed to give water to the United States because of an agreement made in 1944, but Mexico has not been doing this. Texas farmers have lost money because they did not get the water they were supposed to get. The report must list all the government programs that could help these farmers, and it must be finished within six months.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on General Farm Commodities, Risk Management, and Credit.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Texas Agricultural Producers Assistance Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Report on Available Assistance to Agricultural Producers in the State of Texas That Have Suffered Economic Losses Due to the Failure of Mexico to Deliver Water
sections.text: Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the Secretary of Agriculture must send a report to two congressional committees. These committees are the House Committee on Agriculture and the Senate Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry. The report must list all the powers the Secretary has and all the programs in the Department of Agriculture that are available or could be made available to help farmers in Texas. These farmers must have lost money because Mexico failed to deliver water to the United States. Mexico is supposed to deliver this water under a treaty signed in Washington on February third, nineteen forty four. This treaty is called the Treaty Relating to the Utilization of Waters of the Colorado and Tijuana Rivers and of the Rio Grande. There was also an additional agreement signed on November fourteenth, nineteen forty four called the Supplementary Protocol.
